reference_answer: Baylor J. Tregre.
McDonough Marine Service owned barge MARMAC 27 (it was leased by Manson Construction Co.).
DCA24FM038.
$2 million.
A production platform and a helideck.
May 11, 2024, about 0340 (CDT).
Severe thunderstorm watch at 1257 → Coastal Waters Forecast at 1409 → Special Marine Warning at 1501 → Special Marine Warning update at 1544.
Salvage operations were conducted from May 18 through June 15, 2024; the vessel was transported to a salvage dock in Galveston, Texas, on June 12, 2024.
Visibility 0.5 mi; winds southeast 23 kts (with gusts to 48 kts).
Year built 1997; port of registry New Orleans, Louisiana; 97 gross register tons (GRT).
2 × 750 hp (559 kW), Caterpillar 3412 diesel engines.

The mate's inability to maneuver the tow into the wind due to the overwhelming towline force generated by the towed barge during the sudden onset of severe weather, resulting in unrecoverable heeling.
From 1657 (distress call) to 1808 (all four recovered) — about 71 minutes (1 hour 11 minutes).

1,800 feet refers to the length of 1.25-inch tow wire carried on the aft double-drum towing winch (the total wire on the drum). 800 feet refers to the length of the main tow wire (towline) actually paid out between the towing vessel and barge MARMAC 27.

(a) Coast Guard Sector Houston-Galveston received the VHF distress call. (b) Coast Guard District 8 New Orleans received the EPIRB alert. (c) Station Freeport (Texas) launched the response boat. (d) Air Station Houston launched the MH-65 helicopter.
Because the production platform on the barge was too tall to transit under bridge and powerline crossings on the Gulf Intracoastal Waterway.
No. The report states there was no emergency tow-release function in the wheelhouse, nor was one required under 46 CFR Subchapter M.
No. NTSB investigators concluded the fractures and indentations on the superstructure all appeared to be from recovery (salvage) activities, not from the casualty event itself.

No. The Analysis explicitly states that even if the crew had received the Special Marine Warnings, they would not have been able to avoid the storm given the vessel's speed (~4–5 knots), the tow's position (~17 miles from Galveston, surrounded by storms), and the speed of the storm's advance.
No. The April 22, 2024 suitability survey recommended that careful consideration be given to weather criteria for the tow of MARMAC 27, but it did not specify any weather criteria limits.
It was renamed after acquisition. The vessel was built in 1997 as Trent Joseph for Coastal Towing LLC; Trinity Tugs LLC acquired it in 2023 and then renamed it Baylor J. Tregre.
